# flnk usage guide for AI agents

`flnk` is a privacy-first, server-authoritative link shortener that runs as a
single Cloudflare Worker (Next.js App Router compiled through OpenNext). A short
link is a **name**, not a URL: the destination, geo/device routing rules, and
access gates live in D1, are resolved at the edge through a KV read-through
cache, and every click is logged to Cloudflare Analytics Engine with the visitor
IP hashed before storage. It is a lineage rewrite of Sink (export format + the
analytics globe endpoint stay wire-compatible).

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md
- Runtime config (annotated): ./wrangler.jsonc
- Schema: ./src/database/schema.ts

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`; run all commands from the repo root with
`pnpm --filter @cdlab/flnk <script>`.

## What it is

- **One Worker, two surfaces.** Public: `GET/POST /<slug>` (redirect), `GET
  /<slug>/og` (social preview), `GET /m/<slug>` (launchpad page). Console:
  `/dashboard/*` + `/api/*`, gated by a better-auth session.
- **Dual DB driver** selected at request time by `DB_TYPE`: `d1` (the `DB`
  binding) or `libsql` (Turso). Both run in production. Wired via `@cdlab/db/web`.
- **Custom worker entry** `src/worker/index.ts` wraps OpenNext's `fetch` and adds
  a `scheduled()` cron handler (OpenNext emits only `fetch`).

## The redirect pipeline (src/app/[slug]/route.ts)

A `GET /<slug>` flows through fixed guards, each able to short-circuit:

1. reserved-slug guard → 404.
2. per-IP native rate limit (`RESOLVE_RATE_LIMIT`, 100/60s, fails open) → 429.
3. `resolveLink(domain, slug)` — KV `link:{domain}:{slug}` → D1 fallback →
   backfill cache. Returns null → 404.
4. `isExpired` → purge KV + 404.
5. `config.disabled` (paused) → 404.
6. click-cap `config.maxVisits` (KV `visits:{id}`) → disable in D1 + purge + 404.
7. password gate (`config.passwordHash`, Argon2id) — HTML form or `x-link-password`
   header; brute force capped via KV `pwfail:{ip}:{slug}` (5/600s).
8. cloaking / social-crawler-with-OG → serve OG HTML instead of a 3xx.
9. `config.unsafe` → confirm interstitial (chained via a signed gate token).
10. `resolveDestination` — override cascade: geo (`cf.country`) → apple (iOS UA)
    → google (Android UA), then optional `?query` merge (drops `qr`).
11. non-http(s) destination → 404 (open-redirect guard).
12. `writeAccessLog` via `ctx.waitUntil` (non-blocking) → Analytics Engine.
13. 308 (or 303 after a form POST) with `Location: <destination>`.

Key invariants (do not reorder without reading DESIGN §3, §5):

- The lookup key is `(domain, slug)` — same slug ≠ same destination across hosts.
- Password gate runs BEFORE the OG/cloaking branch, or a crawler leaks the
  destination via `og:url`.
- Routing is a last-writer-wins cascade, so a device override beats a geo override.
- The access log records the STORED `link.url`, never the visitor-merged `dest`
  (log-injection guard).
- Form-follow redirects are 303 (force GET), not 307/308.

## Caching (src/lib/data/links/resolve.ts + cache.ts)

KV is a read-through cache in front of D1, key `link:{domain}:{slug}`:

- Positive: resolved link cached `LINK_CACHE_TTL`s (floored to KV's 60s min). A
  link expiring within 60s is left uncached (can't outlive the link).
- Negative: a miss writes a `__miss__` tombstone (`NEGATIVE_CACHE_TTL`s) so
  repeated lookups of a missing slug skip D1 (cache-penetration guard). Malformed
  slugs are shape-rejected before any I/O — no tombstone for junk.
- Invalidation: a create/import overwrites the same key in place; expiry/disable
  purge on the redirect path.

## Data model (src/database/schema.ts)

All business tables share `trackingFields` (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `isDeleted`
soft-delete — NEVER hard-delete; filter `is_deleted=0` or use `@cdlab/db/utils`).

- `links` — `url`, `config` (JSON `LinkConfig`: geo/apple/google, title/desc/image
  OG, cloaking, unsafe, maxVisits, disabled, passwordHash, qr), `tags` (JSON of
  tag **IDs**). Unique `(slug, domain)` — covers soft-deleted rows (revive-in-place).
- `launchpads` — `config` (JSON `LaunchpadConfig`), `og`, `status`
  (draft|published), `ownerId`. `slug` globally unique.
- `tags` — dictionary; links reference IDs so a rename is O(1).
- `user`/`session`/`account`/`verification` — better-auth core tables (names must
  match its adapter).

## Auth (src/lib/platform/auth.ts, with-auth.ts)

- better-auth, social-only (Google + GitHub), login == registration, built lazily
  per isolate.
- `ALLOWED_EMAILS` allow-list gates BOTH first sign-in and every session check
  (tightening it revokes existing sessions). Empty = allow-all (logs a warning).
- Server-side authorization: `requireSession(request)` (API, 401/403),
  `getAllowedSession(headers)` (dashboard layout), `withAuth(schema, handler)`
  (mutation wrapper: session + Zod + uniform `{ error }` envelope). No client-only gate.
- Multi-tenancy is designed-in but a NO-OP today: `scopeToOwner(conds, session)`
  returns conds unchanged (shared workspace). Rows are owner-stamped, so isolation
  flips with no backfill.

## Analytics (src/lib/analytics/)

- Write: `writeAccessLog` emits one AE point — `indexes:[slug]`, 19 blobs
  (blob1..19, mapped in `analytics-query.ts`), `doubles:[lat,lng]`. blob19 `type`
  = link|launchpad|launchpad_block; blob18 `source` = link|qr. Fired via `waitUntil`.
- Privacy: IP stored only as `hex(HMAC-SHA-256(ip:YYYY-MM-DD))[:32]` keyed by
  `ANALYTICS_IP_SALT` (never a public salt; falls back to a key derived from
  `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET`). Daily-rotating, unreversible from public data.
- Read: AE binding only WRITES; dashboard reads use the AE SQL REST API
  (`CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` + a token with Analytics Read). Sampling-weighted
  counts (`SUM(_sample_interval)`). Filter values pass a backslash-safe sanitize.

## API routes (src/app/api/*)

Mutations use `withAuth` (session + Zod); reads use `requireSession`. Public
exceptions: `/api/auth/[...all]`, `/api/launchpad/track` (rate-limited beacon),
`/api/asset/[...key]` (R2 read, `og/`-prefix + image-type restricted).

- `link/*` — create, edit, upsert, delete, import, tag-bulk, tag-set, check
  (health); reads: list, query, search, count, creators, tags, export, ai
  (slug), og-ai.
- `launchpad/*` — create, delete, edit, publish, upsert; list, query, stats; track.
- `stats/*` — counters, views, metrics, export (CSV). `logs/*` — events
  (realtime), locations (globe). `location` — caller's cf coords.
- `upload/image` (R2 write), `asset/[...key]` (R2 read), `backup`, `config`.

Client wrapper: `src/lib/platform/api.ts` (`linkApi`, `launchpadApi`, `statsApi`,
`checkApi`, `migrateApi`, `configApi`, `uploadApi`).

## Config & bindings (wrangler.jsonc, src/lib/platform/env.ts)

- Config via `getConfig()` — Zod-validated once per isolate; precedence: explicit
  env arg → cf context → process.env. Never read env directly on the hot path.
- Bindings: `DB` (D1), `KV`, `ANALYTICS` (Analytics Engine), `AI` (Workers AI,
  optional), `RESOLVE_RATE_LIMIT` (native, optional), `R2` (OPTIONAL, commented
  out — assets + backups degrade gracefully), `ASSETS`.
- Secrets are NEVER vars: `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET`, Google/GitHub client id+secret,
  `ALLOWED_EMAILS`, `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`, `LIBSQL_AUTH_TOKEN`,
  `ANALYTICS_IP_SALT`. Set via `.dev.vars` (local) or `wrangler secret put` (prod).

## Gotchas for agents

- Migrations MUST be generated with the sqlite dialect (`DB_TYPE=d1`) — the libSQL
  default emits `ALTER COLUMN`, which D1 rejects. See DESIGN §10.5.
- `@libsql/client` must stay in `serverExternalPackages` (`next.config.ts`) or the
  OpenNext build breaks (`Could not resolve @libsql/isomorphic-ws`).
- Batch DB writes to ≤100 bound params (D1 cap) — see the import chunking.
- Don't hard-delete; soft-delete via `isDeleted`. The `(slug,domain)` unique index
  covers deleted rows on purpose (revive-in-place).
- Never store a raw IP or add a tracking cookie on the redirect path.

## Common commands

```bash
pnpm --filter @cdlab/flnk dev          # http://flnk.localhost:3355
pnpm --filter @cdlab/flnk cf:localdb   # apply migrations to local D1
pnpm --filter @cdlab/flnk db:gen       # generate a migration from schema.ts
pnpm --filter @cdlab/flnk test         # vitest units
pnpm --filter @cdlab/flnk build        # next build (type-check + bundle)
```
